qa_pairs.question: On the tamIA cluster, what are the characteristics of the different storage spaces available, specifically HOME, SCRATCH, and PROJECT?
qa_pairs.answer: The tamIA cluster offers three main storage spaces. The HOME space is small with fixed per-user quotas and currently has no automatic backup (though one is planned for spring 2026). The SCRATCH space is large, designed for temporary files during computations, has large fixed per-user quotas, lacks automatic backup, and automatically purges old files. The PROJECT space is intended for data sharing among group members and storing large datasets, featuring large adjustable per-project quotas and automatic daily backups.

qa_pairs.question: What are the specifications of the different types of compute nodes available on the tamIA cluster, including their CPU, memory, and GPU configurations?
qa_pairs.answer: The tamIA cluster features three types of compute nodes. There are 12 nodes, each with 64 cores, 1024GB of memory, 2x Intel Xeon Gold 6448Y CPUs, and 8x NVIDIA HGX H200 GPUs (141GB HBM3). Additionally, 53 nodes are available, each with 48 cores, 512GB of memory, 2x Intel Xeon Gold 6442Y CPUs, and 4x NVIDIA HGX H100 GPUs (80GB HBM3). Lastly, there are 8 nodes with 64 cores, 512GB of memory, and 2x Intel Xeon Gold 6438M CPUs, but these nodes do not have GPUs.
